summary: @ts-apis/ts-utils ships an obfuscated credential stealer and C2 implant
details: The package presents itself as a generic TypeScript utility library but dist/index.js contains a charcode-array-obfuscated payload that runs on require(). On import, after a setTimeout delay, it harvests installer secrets from ~/.ssh/ (id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa, config, authorized_keys), ~/.aws/credentials and config, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.git-credentials, gcloud/azure/terraform/vault/solana/railway configs, root.env files in cwd and parent directories, and Kubernetes service-account tokens under /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/ and /run/secrets/. It filters process.env for keys matching KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|AWS|... and queries cloud instance-metadata endpoints (AWS IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254, GCP metadata.google.internal, and ECS task-role credentials at 169.254.170.2) to steal cloud workload identities. The collected data is base64-encoded and POSTed to https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/exfil. It then enters a setInterval loop polling https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/h every 10 seconds for commands: 'KILL' exits, a 'SLIVER'-prefixed response triggers download of a binary to /tmp/.<random>, chmod 755, and detached spawn (Sliver implant loader), and any other response is execSync'd with stdout/stderr returned to the C2. All sensitive strings (hostname, paths, shell commands such as 'cmdkey /list', 'powershell -c Get-ChildItem Env:', 'cat /proc/1/environ', 'ipconfig /all') are encoded as String.fromCharCode arrays to evade scanners. The benign utility-library description and exported helper stubs are cover; the package's real purpose is the implant.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
